memoryArtifacts.headline: 1882. Hulse Family - The History of the County Palatine, Vol 3, Page 464
memoryArtifacts.description:
Page 464, Vol III, 1882.
The History of the County Palatine and City of Chester; Compiled from Original Evidences in Public Offices, The Harleian and Cottonian MSS. , Parochial Registers, Private Muniments, Unpublished MS. Collections of Successive Cheshire Antiquaries, and A personal survey of every township in the county ; Incorporated with a republication of King's Vale Royal, And Leycester's Cheshire Antiquities.
By George Ormerod, Esq. , LL.D. F.R.S. & F.S.A.  
In 3 Volumes, Volume III,
First Edition, 1819 - 472 pages, Second Edition, 1882 
Revised and Enlarged 1882 - 879 Pages
By Thomas Helsby, Esq. , of Lincoln's Inn, Barrister-at-Law.

zh.Norbury by Stockport, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾諾波利 白 斯托普特
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hulse於大約1316年出生在英國爾時，他的父親William De Hulse27歲，母親Enid22歲。他於大約1337年與Sibyl de Norbury在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1342年在英國享年28歲。

zh-hans.Norbury by Stockport, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰爾諾波利 白 斯托普特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Hulse于大约1316年出生在英国爾时，他的父亲William De Hulse27岁，母亲Enid22岁。他于大约1337年与Sibyl de Norbury在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1342年在英国享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hulse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from Hulse in Witton (Cheshire), from Middle English holes, plural of hole ‘hole, hollow, depression’ (Old English holh). See Hole .
North German (Hülse): topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, Middle Low German huls, hüls.
American shortened and altered form of Flemish Holsaert (see Hulsart ). Compare Hulce , Huls , and Hults 1.

variants.name: Hulsart
meanings: Americanized form of Flemish Holsaert: unexplained. See also Hulse 3.

variants.name: Hole
meanings: English (southwestern): topographic name for someone who lived in or by a hole or hollow, from Middle English hol(e) (Old English hol, holh, dative hole), or a habitational name from some minor place so named.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads, so named from the dative singular or indefinite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

In some cases possibly also an American shortened form of Flemish Van Hole: habitational name from the common placename Hol(e), meaning ‘hollow, valley’; or a topographic name from the same term or from Middle Dutch hole, heule ‘(low) arched bridge, weir’.

variants.name: Hults
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin. Compare   Hultz   1.
In some cases also a variant of   Hultz   2, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Hulce
meanings: Altered form of   Hulse  , a surname of Flemish (and also of English and German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hulse was born about 1316, in Cheshire, England, his father, William De Hulse, was 27 and his mother, Enid, was 22. He married Sibyl de Norbury about 1337, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died about 1342, in England, at the age of 28.

Il a épousé Sibyl de Norbury environ 1337, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé environ 1342, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 28 ans.

ko.Norbury by Stockport, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 노르버이 비 스톡포트
ko.lifeSummary: William Hulse(이)가 대략 1316년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Hulse(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Enid(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1337년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Sibyl de Norbury(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1342년 영국에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Sibyl de Norbury aproximadamente 1337, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1342, em Inglaterra, com 28 anos.

Он женился на женщине по имени Sibyl de Norbury около 1337 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер около 1342 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 28 лет.
